data.title: Crypto Market Dynamics Shift As Altcoin Season Fades
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on February 24, CryptoQuant CEO Ki Young Ju shared an analysis indicating that the traditional capital flow cycle of the altcoin season is now outdated. He metaphorically compared the change to climate shifts, stating that the rainy season has vanished, leaving only occasional light showers.

The dominance of Bitcoin in the crypto asset rotation has largely concluded, driven by regulatory developments and institutional adoption. Future capital movements are expected to occur through stablecoins or widely adopted altcoins, marking a departure from the conventional altcoin season.

data.title: dYdX Community Votes On Withdrawal Of Stride Liquidity Staking Plan
data.description: According to Foresight News, the dYdX community has initiated a proposal vote on whether to withdraw from the Stride liquidity staking plan. The proposal currently has a 98.15% approval rate and voting is set to conclude on February 27. The proposal suggests that, following successful testing, the remaining 19,461,949.197705 stDYDX should be transferred to the Treasury SubDAO's control address.

data.title: Stables Labs founder Flex will attend RWAfi Summit to discuss stablecoins, real returns and other topics
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Flex, founder of Stables Labs, the developer of the stablecoin issuance protocol usdx.money, will attend the Plume Denver: RWAfi Summit on February 25. The summit will discuss topics such as RWAfi, stablecoins, and real returns, and connect with leaders in the DeFi field that promote the adoption of tokenized assets.
Flex, founder of Stables Labs, has been active in the fields of cryptocurrency and DeFi for many years. As early as the beginning of the DeFi boom in 2020, he participated in the incubation and construction of multiple related projects. Flex is known for his deep understanding of stablecoin mechanisms and his passion for financial innovation. He started his career in software development and then gradually turned to blockchain technology, focusing on creating solutions that can bridge the gap between traditional finance and decentralized finance.
usdx.money, developed by Stables Labs, is a secure, decentralized, bank-free synthetic stablecoin issuance protocol that focuses on building a new generation of stablecoin infrastructure. By connecting the fields of decentralized finance (DeFi), centralized finance (CeFi), and traditional finance (TradFi), usdx.money provides efficient and stable financial solutions to meet the diverse needs of global users.

data.title: AscendEX will launch Snowfall (CRACK)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News AscendEX (formerly BitMax) will launch Snowfall (CRACK) at 12:00 on February 24, opening the CRACK/USDT trading pair, and is now open for deposits.
AscendEX (formerly BitMax) is the first and most comprehensive trading platform for Memecoin on the entire network. The Meme Research Institute team under the platform selects high-quality Memes for listing based on on-chain data and project background. The platform's Memecoin first launch rate is as high as 80%. Trade Memecoin on AscendEX.

data.title: Bybit CEO: Bybit has fully made up for the ETH shortfall
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou posted an update saying, "Bybit has fully made up for the ETH shortfall, and a new audited proof of asset (POR) report will be released soon, so stay tuned."

data.title: Suspicious Ethereum Contract Transfers 49.5 Million USDC
data.description: According to Foresight News, CertiK Alert has detected suspicious fund transfers involving an unverified contract on the Ethereum network. The address beginning with 0x3ac96 received approximately 49.5 million USDC and subsequently converted it into DAI.

data.title: Donald Trump Jr. To Speak At DeFi World 2025
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of U.S. President Donald Trump, announced on February 24 that he will deliver a speech at DeFi World 2025 on February 26.

data.title: Georgia proposes second Bitcoin reserve bill, allowing the state to invest unlimited amounts in Bitcoin
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Georgia, the United States, has proposed a second Bitcoin reserve bill (SB 228), allowing the state to invest in Bitcoin without any investment restrictions. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs See Significant Inflows and Outflows
data.description:
According to PANews, data from SoSoValue indicates that Ethereum spot ETFs experienced a net inflow of $1.61 million during the trading week from February 18 to February 21, 2025 (Eastern Time). The Fidelity Ethereum ETF (FETH) recorded the highest weekly net inflow among Ethereum spot ETFs, amounting to $26.32 million. This brings FETH's historical total net inflow to $1.54 billion. Conversely, the Grayscale Ethereum Trust (ETHE) saw the largest weekly net outflow, with $15.79 million leaving the fund, contributing to a historical total net outflow of $4 billion.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs stands at $9.981 billion. The net asset ratio, which compares the market value to Ethereum's total market capitalization, is at 3.14%. The cumulative historical net inflow for Ethereum spot ETFs has reached $3.154 billion.

data.title: Hong Kong Asia Holdings Increases Bitcoin Investment
data.description: According to Odaily, Hong Kong-listed company Hong Kong Asia Holdings has announced that its board has approved further investment in Bitcoin. On the 20th of this month, the company conducted a series of transactions on a cryptocurrency trading platform in the open market, acquiring approximately 7.88 units of Bitcoin. The total cost of these transactions was around 5.9369 million Hong Kong dollars, funded through the company's internal resources. Currently, Hong Kong Asia Holdings holds about 8.88 units of Bitcoin, with an average cost of 756,200 Hong Kong dollars per unit.
